commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ea9ed887e1af889cee8fca1acb4433107542d286)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server turn-watch behavior so post-tool assistant item completion waits for `turn/completed`, adds regression coverage, and adds `openai-codex-responses` to the incomplete-turn retry guard.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +101. Total +109 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can treat post-tool assistant `item/completed` as enough to arm assistant-completion release, while upstream Codex documents `turn/completed` as the turn boundary. I did not run a live replay in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because contributor-supplied real behavior proof is missing and the legacy API allowlist change needs removal or explicit maintainer approval.

target.title: fix(codex): wait for turn completion after post-tool handoff
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Codex Harness could terminate a turn too early after a tool handoff. The incorrect boundary was not the content of assistant text itself, but the lifecycle signal used by the native Codex app-server runtime plugin.

Before this patch, once an assistant item completed after tool execution, `assistantCompletionIdleRelease` could release the attempt even though the overall turn had not emitted `turn/completed`. If the assistant item was only an in-progress narration such as “Now let me create ...”, OpenClaw embedded runner could treat that partial item as the final result and return a truncated workspace to the judge.

Codex ACP does not hit the same failure mode because the ACP turn collector records completed assistant items but only finishes the turn on `turn/completed`. Its completion boundary is turn-level, not post-tool assistant-item-level.

## Root Cause

This is an item-level versus turn-level lifecycle mismatch in the Codex Harness path:

- Codex Harness path: OpenClaw embedded runner + native Codex app-server runtime plugin.
- Codex ACP path: ACPX/ACP runtime collector for `agentId=codex`.
- After a tool handoff, assistant `item/completed` is not a reliable final-answer signal. The model may still be continuing the same turn.
- The previous progress-text based workaround was too heuristic because it classified surface text instead of fixing the lifecycle boundary.

## Changes

- Make `isAssistantCompletionReleaseNotification(...)` return `false` after the turn has crossed a tool handoff.
- Document that assistant-completion idle release is only valid before tool handoff; after tool execution, the runtime must wait for `turn/completed` or explicit timeout/failure.
- Remove the embedded-runner progress-only continuation retry workaround and its text-classification rules.
- Add a regression test proving post-tool assistant item completion does not release the turn without `turn/completed`.
- Update existing turn-watch coverage so post-tool completion is driven by `turn/completed`.

## Evidence

Validated locally on the refreshed branch:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-turn-watches.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed
Tests       310 passed
```

Also passed:

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notifications.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts
node scripts/check-plugin-npm-runtime-builds.mjs --package extensions/codex
git diff --check
```

Build result:

```text
checked 1 publishable plugins; built 1 npm runtimes
codex built
```

## Replay Context

The issue was found while comparing OpenClaw 5.28 Codex Harness, Codex ACP, Harness with agent workspace, and OpenClaw PiAgent on the same replay set. The observed Harness failures stopped after a small number of tool rounds with assistant text that described upcoming work rather than a final answer.

This PR intentionally fixes the runtime correctness issue only. CPU utilization differences between Harness and ACP remain a separate measurement topic; the current hypothesis is that Harness performs more local embedded-runner/app-server/tool-bridge/session-mirroring/archive work than ACP.
